page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with a single thread per core. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression are positioned in the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests fall consistently within the average middle 50% range. The server achieves an average rating in database operations and Redis SET operations, processing 10.13 million operations per second.

The high memory density relative to compute resources makes this instance a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound applications that do not demand high single-core processing speeds. The hardware profile and benchmark results indicate that the server is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching layers such as Redis, and general-purpose memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture. Running at 2.5 GHz, this KVM-based server lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show weak single-core performance alongside average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database execution capabilities. It is suited for in-memory databases and caching workloads.
